issues.title: Linking strategy between platform and key tokens
issues.author: ionut-arm
issues.body:
We need to write up a detailed description of how the tokens in the KAT Bundle/EAT collection are (cryptographically) linked together. This might have to build upon the more generic description that will be added to the [EAT collection draft](https://thomas-fossati.github.io/draft-kat/draft-bft-rats-kat.html#I-D.frost-rats-eat-collection).

These details are also relevant to the security of the token and bundle, so should also be mentioned in the Security Considerations section.

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body:
I have a few notes to share:


# evidence composition

the goal is to model layered attestation environments and attestation of composite devices.

two models are discussed, direct and delegated, describing the split of responsibilities between pairs of cooperating attesters, A and B, which are typically arranged in a parent/child relationship reflecting their relative order in the system's chain of trust (CoT)

## direct

in the direct model, attester A entrusts attester B for collecting claims about B's target environment

B does not sign its claims, A does.

## delegated

in the delegated model, attester A entrusts attester B for collecting and signing claims about B's target environment

## cross-evidence binding

a "evidence binder" is a EAT claim that models CoT links between attesting environments.  it encodes the linkage between pairs of evidence produced by different attesters (A and B) in a EAT collection.

(assumption: trust delegation is tree-like)

the high level idea is to sample B's state at a certain point in time and inject an "image" thereof (the binder's value) into A, basically treating B as part of A's target environment

more precisely, the proposal is to:

* select a "representative" subset of B's claims
* wrap their values in a self-delimiting structure
* serialise the wrapping structure into a byte string
* cryptographically digest said byte string
* let A add the computed digest to its target environment

finally, let A and B independently compute evidence for their target environments.

### binding freshness

when either end of the binding operation (A or B) change their state, their binding becomes stale and must be recomputed

### binder description

it may be desirable for simplifying appraisal to explicitly encode the instructions for describing (and therefore verifying) the binding in a claim.
 
to that end, the binding function described in {{cross-evidence-binding}} needs to be specified unambiguously.
 
we make the following assumptions:

* the digest is a known hash function (e.g., one from the COSE algorithms)
* the input is a ordered subset of B's claims
* the output is a claim in the bound token
 
Then B could have a "binder description" claim as follows:

```cddl
; $hash-algo-id($binding-ctx, $claim-key_0, ..., $claim-key_n) => $bound-token-id[$out]
;
token-binder = [
    bind-f: hash-algo-id
    params: [ binding-ctx, + claim-key ]
    dst: [ bound-token-id, claim-key ]
]
 
binding-ctx = text
claim-key = int / text
hash-algo-id = int / text
bound-token-id = int / text
```
 
which may be instantiated as:
 
```
[
  -16, / SHA-256 /
  [
    "key-attestation-token",
    8 / TIK public in cnf /
  ],
  [
    "psa-token", / PSA platform token id in the EAT collection /
    10 / nonce /
  ]
]
```

issues.title: More generic platform attestation token
issues.author: ionut-arm
issues.body: Instead of focusing primarily on PSA Attestation as the main underlying platform token, we should try to abstract that away to generic platform attestation and use PSA Attestation only as an example.

reviews.author: thomas-fossati
reviews.body:
this example is validated against the kat schema (i.e., the naked claims-set) rather than the COSE Sign(1) wrapper.

You can check the validity of the example against the CDDL with `make -C cddl`

reviews.author: thomas-fossati
reviews.body: nice!  A couple of comments inlined.

comments.body: This bit should be moved to the bottom of the Terminology section.

ASCII can be automatically rendered as SVG:

```suggestion
~~~ aasvg
```
since KAK is a AK and AK is defined as belonging to the RoT, I guess the RoT is also supposed to attest to the quality and security (integrity, secrecy) of the IK.

reviews.author: ionut-arm
reviews.body: Thank you! A few comments below

comments.body: Do we need a link here to the RATS draft?

```suggestion
key of the IK (skT). How this proof-of-possession of the private key is accomplished
depends on the details of the usage protocol and is outside the scope of this specification.
```

```suggestion
The Recipient of the CAB and the proof-of-possession data (such as a digital signature)
```

Regarding the freshness - do we want to convey the option of "PAT generated at initialisation and then cached", where only the KAT includes the freshness? Also, I don't think it's specified that the RP is the one providing the nonce, but perhaps that can be assumed
Do we also need to specify the replay check for the KAT?
@ionut-arm, do you think we could convert this into an issue to be handled subsequently and merge as-is?
Sure!

comments.body:

```suggestion
Security-sensitive functionality, like attestation, has to be placed into
```

```suggestion
whereby platform attestation requires more privilege than the key attestation code.
```

```suggestion
usage protocol. The usage protocol, which is outside the scope of this specification, needs to
```
just a curiosity, what does `T` means in `[ps]kT` ?

IIUC, the bundling method we describe reuses the PAT across multiple KATs, so the session freshness for the "usage protocol" is to be checked in the KAT only.

This step should be "PAT and KAT chaining is checked"
The Name Key Attestation itself is mis-leading
It should be better termed as "Augmented Key Attestation" or "Enhanced Key Attestation"

> It should be better termed as "Augmented Key Attestation" or "Enhanced Key Attestation"

not sure, we are not enhancing or augmenting key attestation

> The Name Key Attestation itself is mis-leading

in what sense?
We are not documenting Key Attestation here!
